name: GTM Starter Pack
description: Combined outreach + review templates for founder-led GTM

prompts.title: Founder-Led Sales: First 20 Conversations
prompts.prompt:
Create a founder sales playbook for first 20 discovery calls for {{PRODUCT}} at {{PRICE_POINT}}.

Structure per call:
1. Pre-call (5 mins) - LinkedIn, site, tech stack, trigger event
2. Opening (3 mins) - Context, agenda, permission to ask hard questions
3. Discovery (25 mins) - Situation, Pain, Impact, Critical Event, Decision (SPICED)
4. Demo/Walkthrough (10 mins) - Only what maps to their pain
5. Next Steps (5 mins) - Clear owner, date, deliverable

Includes:
- 10 discovery questions (ranked by signal)
- 5 disqualification criteria (walk away early)
- Pricing conversation script (anchor high, justify)
- Follow-up email template (sent within 1h)
- CRM fields to capture (deal stage, MEDDIC, next action)
prompts.notes: Founder does first 20. No sales hire until 3 closes at target price. Record every call (Fathom/Grain). Review weekly.

prompts.title: ICP Refinement + Account List Builder
prompts.prompt:
Build a systematic ICP refinement + account list process for {{PRODUCT}}.

Phase 1: Define ICP attributes (firmographic + technographic + behavioral)
- Company size, revenue, geo
- Tech stack signals (e.g., uses Stripe, Segment, Snowflake)
- Behavioral triggers (hiring X role, raised funding, launched product)

Phase 2: Score & prioritize (1-5 each)
- Pain severity
- Budget authority
- Urgency
- Accessibility

Phase 3: Build list of 100 accounts
- Apollo/Clay/LinkedIn Sales Nav
- Enrich: decision maker, email, trigger
- Tag: Tier 1 (20), Tier 2 (30), Tier 3 (50)

Phase 4: Weekly motion
- 20 new Tier 1/Tier 2 contacts/week
- 5 referrals/week from network
- Track: reply rate, meeting rate, SQL rate

Output: Notion/Airtable template + weekly dashboard.
prompts.notes: ICP = hypothesis. Refine weekly based on who replies and closes. Kill Tier 3 if Tier 1 conversion >20%.

prompts.title: Pricing Page + Packaging Generator
prompts.prompt:
Generate a pricing page structure and packaging logic for {{PRODUCT_TYPE}}.

Framework:
1. Anchor: Enterprise/High tier first (price, value)
2. Middle: Growth/Pro (target 60% of buyers)
3. Entry: Starter/Free (qualifier, not revenue driver)

Each tier:
- Headline outcome (not feature list)
- 3-5 value bullets
- One "best for" line
- Price (monthly + annual)
- CTA: "Start trial" / "Talk to sales"

Packaging levers:
- Seats vs usage vs outcomes
- Feature gates (SSO, API, SLA, support)
- Implementation included?

Output: HTML/CSS-ready copy + Stripe price ID mapping table.
prompts.notes: Test with 5 buyers before launch. "Would you pay $X for this outcome?" not "Do you like this pricing?"

prompts.title: Weekly GTM Review Dashboard
prompts.prompt:
Create a weekly GTM review dashboard template for founder/operator.

Metrics (update every Monday):
- Top of funnel: New contacts, reply rate, meeting booked
- Pipeline: SQLs, opps created, weighted pipeline
- Conversion: SQL→Opp, Opp→Close, cycle time
- Revenue: New ARR, expansion, churn, net new
- Efficiency: CAC, LTV:CAC, payback period
- Activity: Calls, emails, demos, proposals sent

Format:
- Rolling 4-week trend
- Red/amber/green vs target
- One-sentence narrative per section
- 3 decisions needed this week

Tools: Notion + Supabase/Postgres + Grafana or Notion charts.
prompts.notes: Review Monday 9am. 30 mins max. Decisions > metrics. If no decision from a metric, stop tracking it.

prompts.title: Starter Outreach + Operator Workbook (Full Content)
prompts.prompt:
This pack includes ALL prompts from Starter Outreach Pack ($79) AND Operator Workbook Pack ($129) at a bundle discount.

See individual pack files for full prompt content.

Bonus: GTM-specific integrations:
- Outreach sequences mapped to RPM checklist gates
- Objection handling → Incident retrospective patterns
- Referral requests → Hiring loop for first sales hire
- ICP refinement → Vendor evaluation for sales tools
prompts.notes: Best value for solo founders doing everything. Use Outreach for top-of-funnel, Workbook for execution discipline.
